PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Jan-24-2018 Continued To Feb-21-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Lon Lazar for and with Renard Williams (Petitioner).
Ben Fox appeared by telephone for Bridgette Carter.

Mr. Lazar stated that he has had several discussions with Counsel, that there is mistrust amongst the Siblings, that he feels Mr. Williams is appropriate to serve, that the main asset is a piece of real property in San Francisco, that his Client lives in San Francisco and that there are some title issues. Mr. Fox stated that the problem is Mr. Williams was involved in collecting rent and did not provide it to the Decedent, and that $1,600.00 a month has been collected since 2001. Mr. Lazar stated that rental income started being received in 2005, that it is a single family home, that his client lived in the house with his Grandfather, that with his Father's blessing his Client began to rent the property, that the Father wanted nothing to do with the property, that his Client has kept the property up and has done maintenance, and that the Sibling had full knowledge for many, many years. Mr. Williams stated that he moved out of the property in 2005, that his Father refused to get involved with the rental of the property and that everything he got out of the house went back into the house. Mr. Williams also stated that there are two tenants, that rents are going into an estate account, that his Dad passed away in October, and that after his Father's death, his Sister went to his Father's bank and took out the money. The Court raised the issue of having a neutral third party serve. Mr. Fox stated that there has been no subsequent discussion as to a neutral party, that Mr. Williams collected $250,000.00 in rents, and there is no way Mr. Williams can take a neutral position as the personal representative and evaluate those claims. Both Petitions for Letters of Administration are continued to February 21, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for the parties to discuss a neutral third party. If there is no agreement, the Court will rule on the petitions.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.
